4. Accessories sold separately.

5. Trade‑in values will vary based on the condition, year, and configuration of your eligible trade‑in device. Not all devices are eligible for credit. You must be at least the age of majority to be eligible to trade in for credit or for an Apple Gift Card. Trade‑in value may be applied toward qualifying new device purchase or added to an Apple Gift Card. Actual value awarded is based on receipt of a qualifying device matching the description provided when estimate was made. Sales tax may be assessed on full value of a new device purchase. In‑store trade‑in requires presentation of a valid photo ID (local law may require saving this information). Offer may not be available in all stores and may vary between in‑store and online trade‑in. Some stores may have additional requirements. Apple or its trade‑in partners reserve the right to refuse, cancel, or limit quantity of any trade‑in transaction for any reason. More details are available from Apple’s trade‑in partner for trade‑in and recycling of eligible devices. Restrictions and limitations may apply.

6. Apple Card Monthly Installments (ACMI) is a 0% APR payment option that is only available if you select it at checkout in the U.S. for eligible products purchased at Apple Store locations, apple.com, the Apple Store app, or by calling 1-800-MY-APPLE, and is subject to credit approval and credit limit. See here for more information about eligible products. Existing customers: See your Customer Agreement for your variable APR. As of January 1, 2026, the variable APR on new Apple Card accounts ranges from 17.49% to 27.74%. You must elect to use ACMI at checkout. If you buy an ACMI‑eligible product with a one‑time payment on Apple Card at checkout, that purchase is subject to your Apple Card’s variable APR, not the ACMI 0% APR. Taxes and shipping on items purchased using ACMI are subject to your Apple Card’s variable APR, not the ACMI 0% APR. In order to buy an iPhone with ACMI, you must select one of the following carriers (prepaid carrier plans are not supported): AT&T, Boost Mobile, T‑Mobile, or Verizon. An iPhone purchased with ACMI is always unlocked, so you can switch carriers at any time, subject to your carrier’s terms. ACMI is not available for purchases made at the following special storefronts or when using these discounts in‑store at Apple: Apple Employee Purchase Plan; participating corporate Employee Purchase Programs; Apple at Work for small businesses; Government and Veterans and Military Purchase Programs; or on refurbished devices. The last month’s payment for each product will be the product’s purchase price, less all other payments at the monthly payment amount. ACMI is subject to change at any time for any reason, including but not limited to installment term lengths and eligible products. See the Apple Card Customer Agreement for more information about ACMI.

To access and use all Apple Card features and products available only to Apple Card users, you must add Apple Card to Wallet on an iPhone or iPad that supports and has the latest version of iOS or iPadOS. Apple Card is subject to credit approval, available only for qualifying applicants in the United States, and issued by Goldman Sachs Bank USA, Salt Lake City Branch.

Apple Payments Services LLC, a subsidiary of Apple Inc., is a service provider of Goldman Sachs Bank USA for Apple Card and Savings accounts. Neither Apple Inc. nor Apple Payments Services LLC is a bank.

All communications from Apple and Goldman Sachs Bank USA about Apple Card (including transactional and marketing communications) and customer service support are available in English. Certain communications about Apple Card can be viewed in another language depending on your device language settings. If you reside in the U.S. Virgin Islands, American Samoa, Guam, Northern Mariana Islands, or U.S. Minor Outlying Islands, please call Goldman Sachs at 877-255-5923 with questions about Apple Card.

1. Testing conducted by Apple in January 2026 using preproduction MacBook Neo systems with Apple A18 Pro, 6‑core CPU, 5‑core GPU, 8GB of unified memory, and 256GB SSD. Wireless web battery life tested by browsing 25 popular websites while connected to Wi‑Fi. Video streaming battery life tested with 1080p content in Safari while connected to Wi‑Fi. All systems tested with display brightness set to 8 clicks from bottom and keyboard backlight off. Battery life varies by use and configuration. See apple.com/batteries for more information.

2. Screen size is measured diagonally. The display has rounded corners at the top. When measured as a standard rectangular shape, the screen is 13.0 inches diagonally (actual viewable area is less).

3. Testing conducted by Apple in January 2026 using preproduction 13‑inch MacBook Air systems with Apple M5, 10‑core CPU, and 8‑core GPU, and preproduction 15‑inch MacBook Air systems with Apple M5, 10‑core CPU, and 10‑core GPU, all configured with 16GB of unified memory and 512GB SSD. Wireless web battery life tested by browsing 25 popular websites while connected to Wi‑Fi. Video streaming battery life tested with 1080p content in Safari while connected to Wi‑Fi. All systems tested with display brightness set to 8 clicks from bottom and keyboard backlight off. Battery life varies by use and configuration. See apple.com/batteries for more information.

4. Display is measured diagonally as a standard rectangular shape. Actual viewable area is slightly less due to the rounded corners at the top.

5. 1GB = 1 billion bytes and 1TB = 1 trillion bytes; actual formatted capacity less.

6. Battery life varies by use and configuration; see apple.com/batteries for more information.

7. Screen size is measured diagonally.

8. Trade‑in values will vary based on the condition, year, and configuration of your eligible trade‑in device. Not all devices are eligible for credit. You must be at least the age of majority to be eligible to trade in for credit or for an Apple Gift Card. Trade‑in value may be applied toward qualifying new device purchase, or added to an Apple Gift Card. Actual value awarded is based on receipt of a qualifying device matching the description provided when estimate was made. Sales tax may be assessed on full value of a new device purchase. In‑store trade‑in requires presentation of a valid photo ID (local law may require saving this information). Offer may not be available in all stores, and may vary between in‑store and online trade‑in. Some stores may have additional requirements. Apple or its trade‑in partners reserve the right to refuse, cancel, or limit quantity of any trade‑in transaction for any reason. More details are available from Apple’s trade‑in partner for trade‑in and recycling of eligible devices. Restrictions and limitations may apply.

9. Education pricing is available to current and newly accepted college students and their parents, as well as faculty, staff, and homeschool teachers of all grade levels. For more information, visit apple.com/us-edu/store.

10. Apple Card Monthly Installments (ACMI) is a 0% APR payment option that is only available if you select it at checkout in the U.S. for eligible products purchased at Apple Store locations, apple.com, the Apple Store app, or by calling 1-800-MY-APPLE, and is subject to credit approval and credit limit. See here for more information about eligible products. Existing customers: See your Customer Agreement for your variable APR. As of January 1, 2026, the variable APR on new Apple Card accounts ranges from 17.49% to 27.74%. You must elect to use ACMI at checkout. If you buy an ACMI‑eligible product with a one‑time payment on Apple Card at checkout, that purchase is subject to your Apple Card’s variable APR, not the ACMI 0% APR. Taxes and shipping on items purchased using ACMI are subject to your Apple Card’s variable APR, not the ACMI 0% APR. In order to buy an iPhone with ACMI, you must select one of the following carriers (prepaid carrier plans are not supported): AT&T, Boost Mobile, T‑Mobile, or Verizon. An iPhone purchased with ACMI is always unlocked, so you can switch carriers at any time, subject to your carrier’s terms. ACMI is not available for purchases made at the following special storefronts or when using these discounts in‑store at Apple: Apple Employee Purchase Plan; participating corporate Employee Purchase Programs; Apple at Work for small businesses; Government and Veterans and Military Purchase Programs; or on refurbished devices. The last month’s payment for each product will be the product’s purchase price, less all other payments at the monthly payment amount. ACMI is subject to change at any time for any reason, including but not limited to installment term lengths and eligible products. See the Apple Card Customer Agreement for more information about ACMI.

To access and use all Apple Card features and products available only to Apple Card users, you must add Apple Card to Wallet on an iPhone or iPad that supports and has the latest version of iOS or iPadOS. Apple Card is subject to credit approval, available only for qualifying applicants in the United States, and issued by Goldman Sachs Bank USA, Salt Lake City Branch.

Apple Payments Services LLC, a subsidiary of Apple Inc., is a service provider of Goldman Sachs Bank USA for Apple Card and Savings accounts. Neither Apple Inc. nor Apple Payments Services LLC is a bank.

All communications from Apple and Goldman Sachs Bank USA about Apple Card (including transactional and marketing communications) and customer service support are available in English. Certain communications about Apple Card can be viewed in another language depending on your device language settings. If you reside in the U.S. Virgin Islands, American Samoa, Guam, Northern Mariana Islands, or U.S. Minor Outlying Islands, please call Goldman Sachs at 877-255-5923 with questions about Apple Card.

results.subject: Fw: ACT Family Upcoming Information
results.raw_body:
Hi verta, can you create events from following mail?


Get Outlook for iOS
From: Tuupo, LeAnn <TuupoL@issaquah.wednet.edu>
Sent: Thursday, February 26, 2026 3:22 PM
To: Tuupo, LeAnn <TuupoL@issaquah.wednet.edu>
Subject: ACT Family Upcoming Information
 

ACT Families,

 

I can’t believe we are at the end of February already. Time flies when we are having fun here at ACT! Second Semester is up and running smooth. Students are really engaging well in their new internships and are really showing their life and vocational skills we work on. Many of our students participated in the Special Olympics Basketball team and it is exciting to share that the team is headed to the state competition in Wenatchee this weekend!

(If your student will be leaving early or not attending ACT tomorrow, please make sure to email the case manager and/or myself.)

 

I want to make you aware of coming events:

 

Friday Feb 27th & Friday March 6th

We have a coming outing this Friday and next Friday. We are headed to the movies. Students will be seeing GOAT at our local Regal theater in Issaquah. Here is the link to the trailer: https://kidszone.sonypictures.com/movies/goat

The students are focused on Things to do in our Community. We are utilizing our social skills as we practice using money through budgeting for our tickets and any snacks. Group A will go this week and Group B will go next Friday. Students that stay on campus will be learning about things to do at the local community center, downtown Seattle and a virtual field trip to the Warner Brother’s Studio to see how animation films are made.

 

Please make sure your ACT student has $15 in CASH in their wallet as we need to purchase tickets as a collective group.

If you need assistance with the funds for the outing, please let me know. Thank you to the Issaquah Schools Foundation, as they have given a grant fund to support our community outings and curriculum field trips.

 

Mark your calendar for Tuesday, March 10th 5:30-6:30 pm

We will have a 2nd Semester ACT Curriculum Night. Our teachers and staff will share what our focus is here at ACT for the curriculum and instruction in vocational skills, our life skills focus and our internship pathways for students over their time here at ACT. We will meet in the Green Classroom. If you are not able to attend, I will share the information presented with all families in the following newsletter.

 

Friday March 13th NO SCHOOL Weather Make-up Day

At this time we have not had a school closure for inclement weather. This means that we will have no school on March 13th as this was held for a weather make-up day.

 

The latter part of March and first week of April will have our community accessing another activity in the community, painting pottery. Students will also need $15 in cash for this outing as we will purchase the pottery items ahead of time to ensure they get the piece they want. We will be splitting into 3 groups for this outing. More information to come soon on that.

 

Thank you for all you do to partner with our team. We appreciate the communication, support at home and the amazing community of families we have here at ACT. I look forward to seeing you all on March 10th, if not sooner.

 

In partnership,

 

LeAnn Tuupo

Principal on Special Assignment

Academy for Community Transition

results.subject: Tailscale Winter Update 2026 is here
results.raw_body:
At Tailscale, we believe secure connectivity should make things easier to run, easier to think through, and harder to mess up, even as environments get more dynamic. Enter: Winter Update 2026.

Hi there,

 

At Tailscale, we believe secure connectivity should make things easier to run, easier to think through, and harder to mess up, even as environments get more dynamic. Enter: Winter Update 2026.

 

Starting tomorrow, we're rolling out a set of updates that reflect how quickly the world around secure connectivity is changing. It’s a week of shipping, showing, and connecting the dots, capped with a look at what it all adds up to. We're focused on: 

  • How modern AI systems can move fast with secure, identity-aware access
  • How teams connect CI/CD pipelines and cloud workloads using native, keyless identity
  • How businesses control and audit privileged infrastructure access with full visibility
  • How to maintain reliable, high-throughput connectivity even in constrained environments
  • How to build on Tailscale as a platform without compromising security

Follow along & join in

  • Email: We'll be sending you daily updates (for this week only) as we launch exciting new features. If you'd prefer not to receive these updates, let us know.
  • Discord: Join CEO Avery Pennarun and VP of Customer Experience Travis Perkins on Discord as they recap this week's announcements on Friday at 4 PM ET / 1 PM PT.

  • Blog: Get each day’s post as it goes live (read the first post).

  • YouTube: Watch short demos and recaps on our YouTube channel.

  • Social: Follow us on Mastodon, Bluesky, or LinkedIn for updates and clips.

And don’t miss our live webinar on Thursday, February 19 at 1 PM ET, where we’ll walk through how the pieces fit together and what’s next for Tailscale.

 

See you online,

 

Ross Kukulinski

VP of Product, Tailscale

Hi there,

 

We hope you're enjoying using Tailscale so far! We want to make sure you get the most out of our product, and the best way to do that is to add devices to your network.

 

Tailscale can network all the devices in your life — way beyond your computers and phones. Whether you’re trying to install it on your game console or even your vacuum cleaner, Tailscale takes your connectivity to a whole other level.

results.subject: 3 easy steps to enhanced connectivity
results.raw_body:
View in browser

Hi there,

 

Getting started with Tailscale is simple. Start by downloading the app, then follow 3 quick steps:

  1. Add your devices
    Tailscale works seamlessly with Linux, Windows, macOS, Raspberry Pi, Android, Synology, and more. Log in to connect your devices to your network.
  2. Share with your network
    We believe in small, interconnected, personal networks. If you have a team you are working with, ask them to install Tailscale to instantly connect in the same organization or log in to send an invite by email.
  3. Explore key features
    Share files between devices and across operating systems with Taildrop, connect to an existing network with subnet routers, control user and device access with ACLs, share services outside of your domain with node sharing, and more!
    Get started →

    Need help? Check out our documentation or read through our Support options.

     

    Cheers,
